overall_cve.delta_vs_prior_run: April MTD moved 3698 (day 20 snapshot) → 3885 (day 21 snapshot): +187 CVEs net over 24h. Most of that growth is NVD back-population to 2026-04-20: that day's finalized count is now 166 (vs 14 carried forward by yesterday's same-day query). Apr 21 alone added 35 CVEs. Full-month extrapolation essentially flat: 5547 → 5550. Daily rate still ~185/day. W17 partial (days 1-2) = 201.

kev_additions.reconciliation_note: Four-day dry spell ended 2026-04-20 with a batch of 8 new entries, lifting April total from 14 → 22. April now above the Q1 monthly average (~24) with nine days remaining.

CVE-2023-27351 — PaperCut NG/MF print server (2026-04-20) — legacy/aged CVE now KEV-listed
CVE-2025-32975 — Quest KACE Systems Management Appliance (2026-04-20)
CVE-2024-27199 — JetBrains TeamCity (2026-04-20) — CI/CD pipeline path auth bypass
notable_entries: CVE-2026-20122 / 20128 / 20133 — Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager cluster. Cisco is a Glasswing participant and these are HTTP-parsing-adjacent bugs on the network-management web plane; bears further investigation for possible probable-participant-self-scan classification (analyst remit). However, KEV listing implies active exploitation before disclosure, which would disqualify them from that bucket per current criteria.

CVE-2026-34197 — Apache ActiveMQ (2026-04-16) — per Horizon3.ai disclosure, found by Naveen Sunkavally using Claude (~10 min attack-chain trace). Claude-assisted but NOT directly credited to Anthropic/Carlini.

confirmed_glasswing: CVE-2026-4747 (FreeBSD NFS RCE, ~17-year-old bug — Anthropic/Carlini autonomous find)
adjacent_claude_credited: CVE-2026-5194 (wolfSSL cert validation — Mythos Preview-assisted, Carlini @ Anthropic)
claude_assisted_non_anthropic: CVE-2026-34197 (Apache ActiveMQ — Horizon3.ai / Naveen Sunkavally using Claude as tool; NOT direct Anthropic credit)
glasswing_cves.source: VulnCheck tracker + The Register 2026-04-15; Calif.io MADBugs program; Tenable/InfoQ coverage. No new direct-attribution CVEs detected in this run.
glasswing_cves.notes: Holding official count at 40 pending additional direct-attribution CVE IDs. Carlini-claimed 'five Linux kernel vulnerabilities' via Claude Code remain unenumerated in public advisories beyond CVE-2026-4747. Today's Cisco SD-WAN Manager KEV cluster is NOT being counted toward Glasswing (active-exploitation evidence means it fails the 'no prior attacker exploitation' criterion for probable-participant-self-scan).

ms_patch_tuesday.source: Tenable blog, Krebs on Security, MSRC (value unchanged since 2026-04-14 release)
ms_patch_tuesday.ranking: Second-largest Patch Tuesday ever per Redmondmag; shy of October 2025 (167).

rhel8_errata.source: Estimate retained from prior run. stack.watch parent page continues to return only YTD aggregate. Red Hat security data API still 404s.
rhel8_errata.confidence: low — placeholder; true April count likely 20-30 but not precisely determinable from available sources

weekly_cve.observation: W17 opens hotter than W16 at ~101/day over the first two days (201 total). The key driver is NVD finalizing Apr 20 at 166 entries (vs 14 at the point-in-time we snapshotted yesterday) — classic back-population. Apr 21 itself carried 35 same-day entries. If W17 continues at ~100/day it will land ~700 for the week, lower still than W16; but back-population typically lifts full-week counts by 20-40% vs same-day reads. Too early to call an inflection.

projection_comparison.commentary: April 2026 extrapolates to ~5,550 — ~500 below the conservative May anchor (6,050) and ~2,000 below the aggressive May anchor (7,550). Extrapolation is effectively flat vs yesterday (5547 → 5550) despite +187 CVEs landing, because most of that was back-population to completed days rather than new day 21 publishing. April is tracking ~12% below March 2026 (6,304). No Glasswing volume inflection visible yet. Counter-argument: NVD publishing latency (4–8 weeks) means Glasswing-era CVEs from the April 7 launch largely haven't surfaced yet; reduced weekend/back-half-of-month publishing is a known NVD cadence artifact. Expect the first meaningful volume signal in May–June 2026, with fuller visibility after Anthropic's planned July disclosure.

counter_arguments: CVE volume has NOT spiked post-Glasswing launch (April 7) through day 21: April extrapolates to ~5,550, ~12% BELOW March's 6,304 and well below all +500 to +5,000/mo projections. Two reads: (a) NVD publishing latency is 4–8 weeks so Glasswing discoveries may not appear yet; (b) Glasswing disclosures are being staged through coordinated vendor patches, so they'll arrive lumpily rather than as a smooth monthly trend.

Daily publication rate has stabilized near ~185/day. Today's +187 over 24h is a back-population artifact, not a real acceleration — Apr 20 alone moved from 14 to 166 as NVD caught up. The underlying pace is still well below the aggressive +2-5k/mo thesis.

The March 2026 spike to 6,304 (+57.1% YoY vs March 2025) clearly predates Glasswing and is driven by CNA expansion, automated fuzzing, and broader disclosure norms — not AI discovery. This is the right baseline for measuring Glasswing's volume impact.

CISA KEV April additions jumped 14 → 22 today on a Cisco SD-WAN Manager batch. Still pacing near the Q1 average (~24/mo) rather than accelerating. KEV additions are editorial decisions by CISA, not purely volume-driven. Five of 22 April entries are legacy re-adds (2009, 2012, 2020, 2023 CVEs) reflecting CISA catalog housekeeping, not new exploitation.

Today's Cisco SD-WAN Manager cluster (CVE-2026-20122/20128/20133) is interesting on the probable-participant-self-scan thesis — Cisco is a Glasswing participant and these are HTTP-adjacent management-plane bugs — but KEV inclusion implies active exploitation before disclosure, which fails the 'no known attacker exploitation' qualification criterion. We are NOT counting them toward either Glasswing-attributed or probable-participant-self-scan buckets for that reason.
Microsoft's 163-CVE April Patch Tuesday is notable but a single data point; October 2025 already hit 167 pre-Glasswing, so high-volume Patch Tuesdays aren't novel.

Weekly CVE data: W14 186/day, W15 231/day, W16 168/day (final), W17 partial ~101/day over 2 days (back-population expected to lift this). W16 — the first FULL week post-Glasswing — came in as the slowest of the three measured weeks. If Glasswing were already inflating publication volume, we'd expect W16 to be higher, not lower, than W14.

Other side of the argument: CVE-2026-34197 (Apache ActiveMQ, KEV-confirmed) was disclosed 2026-04-07 and confirmed by Horizon3.ai using Claude — that's Claude finding a high-impact, actively-exploited vuln in ~10 minutes, independently of Anthropic's own Glasswing research. If we count 'Claude-assisted vulns that end up on KEV' rather than 'CVEs directly credited to Anthropic', the population is already larger than 40 and will likely grow faster than the headline Glasswing count.
